_note: VERIFY ME. First-pass transcription of ASL Chapter C To Hit (C3) and To Kill (C7) for the deterministic TH/TK calculator. The NUMBERS here (basic TH#, range DRM, base TK#, ammo modifiers) are a best-effort starting point and MUST be checked against the eASLRB cell-by-cell, exactly like ift_table.json was human-verified. The STRUCTURE is data-driven: fixing a value = editing this file, no code change. Conventions: TH/TK numbers are the 2d6 total you must roll AT OR UNDER to succeed; higher = easier. DRM follows ASL sign convention: positive DRM = harder (added to the dr / subtracted from the TH#). Range is bracketed; range_drm and the TK rows are indexed left-to-right against `range_brackets`.

to_hit._note: Final TH# = basic[target_type] - (range_drm[weapon_type][bracket] + ammo_drm[ammo] + user Hit Determination Modifiers). A To Hit dr of 2 always hits; 12 always misses.

to_kill._note: Base TK# by caliber row (mm) x range bracket. Final TK# = base + ammo_mod[ammo][bracket]. Target Armor is NOT subtracted here (no armor input) - the user applies armor themselves (each armor point = -1 to the TK#). Smoke cannot kill. Caliber rows are thresholds: a weapon's mm uses the largest row <= its mm.
